message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    There is an imperative need to determine the durability of adaptive immunity to SARS-CoV-2. We enumerated SARS-CoV-2-reactive CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells targeting S1 and M proteins and measured RBD-specific serum IgG over a period of 2-6 months after symptoms onset in a cohort of subjects who had recovered from severe clinical forms of COVID-19.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We recruited 58 patients (38 males and 20 females; median age, 62.5 years), who had been hospitalized with bilateral pneumonia, 60% with one or more comorbidities. IgG antibodies binding to SARS-CoV-2 RBD were measured by ELISA. SARS-CoV-2-reactive CD69
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    -expressing-IFNγ-producing-CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells were enumerated in heparinized whole blood by flow cytometry for ICS.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Detectable SARS-CoV-2-S1/M-reactive CD69
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    -IFN-γ CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells were displayed in 17 (29.3%) and 6 (10.3%) subjects respectively, at a median of 84 days after onset of symptoms (range, 58-191 days). Concurrent comorbidities increased the risk (OR, 3.15; 95% CI, 1.03-9.61;
                    <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                    =0.04) of undetectable T-cell responses in models adjusted for age, sex and hospitalization ward. Twenty-one out of the 35 patients (60%) had detectable RBD-specific serum IgGs at a median of 118 days (range, 60 to 145 days) after symptoms onset. SARS-CoV-2 RBD-specific IgG serum levels were found to drop significantly over time.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    A relatively limited number of subjects who developed severe forms of COVID-19 had detectable SARS-CoV-2-S1/M IFNγ CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells at midterm after clinical diagnosis. Our data also indicated that serum levels of RBD-specific IgGs decline over time, becoming undetectable in some patients.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Adaptive immune responses to SARS-CoV-2 in recovered severe COVID-19 patients

reference.article-title: Overview of Immune Response During SARS-CoV-2 Infection: Lessons From the Past
